body:
## Summary
- Default packaged automation-mode wiring to `agent` when `AGENT_AUTOMATION_MODE` is unset.
- Default project-management label application to enabled while preserving `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_DRY_RUN=true` as the write guard.
- Update docs and focused regression tests for the new defaults.

## Tests
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/apply-project-management-labels-cli.test.js`

Closes #155

comments.body:
Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| implement | review | PR #157 | 3 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced open PR #157 for issue #155, so the next useful automated step is review.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777965037004 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUzNjI1NzIyNDk6aW1wbGVtZW50OjE1NTpyZXZpZXc6MTU3OjM -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | The default changes are reflected in `.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md`, `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md`, and `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md`, including the important note that project-label writes remain blocked by dry-run by default. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The implementation reuses existing workflow inputs/env plumbing and the existing `boolEnv("AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_APPLY_LABELS", ...)` CLI path rather than adding a parallel config mechanism. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is narrowly scoped to requested defaults, aligned docs, and focused tests. It changes workflow fallback/default values, one CLI fallback, and assertions covering those contracts. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The behavior is implemented as direct fallback/default flips from `disabled`/`false` to `agent`/`true`, matching issue #155 without extra branching or new abstractions. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | pass | 7/7 | Project label application now defaults enabled, but dry-run remains the default write guard in docs and workflow config; the CLI still parses and validates the managed-label plan before reporting a dry-run/no-apply status. |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | The PR changes packaged automation mode defaults to `agent`, but direct implement/review/fix-pr leaf workflows still only run handoff steps when `orchestration_enabled == 'true'`; the explicit `/orchestrate` chain from issue #155 is the case intended to continue. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR body closes #155, whose body requested exactly these two default flips, and the issue discussion explicitly called out docs/tests plus preserving dry-run protection. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric drivers are that the PR implements the exact linked request, keeps dry-run as the safety boundary, and updates docs/tests with the default changes.
- Route authorization, merge strategy, status-comment formatting, rubric-authoring, and idempotent artifact rubrics were not scored because this PR does not materially change those behaviors.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric violations found among the applicable rubrics.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #157 implements issue #155’s requested default changes for Sepo automation configuration.
- `AGENT_AUTOMATION_MODE` now defaults to `agent` instead of the previous disabled/heuristics path.
- `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_APPLY_LABELS` now defaults to `true`, while `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_DRY_RUN` remains enabled by default as the write guard.
- The PR also updates docs and focused regression tests to keep the user-facing configuration contract aligned.

## Review
The independent reviews agree the implementation is correct, surgical, documented, and covered by tests. No blocking or warning-level issues were identified, and no inline comments were posted because there were no actionable findings tied to changed lines.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues | INFO | Reviewers found the requested default flips complete and consistent. One reviewer noted optional wording polish for the unchanged router workflow description, but it does not require branch work. |

## Progress
- Issue #155’s two requested default changes are implemented.
- Dry-run protection for project-management label writes remains intact.
- Rubrics review already passed with no required follow-up.
- Existing PR discussion has no inline review comments, and no duplicate feedback needed to be posted.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: No unresolved required branch-change work remains, so automation should stop and leave merge timing to maintainers.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #157.

no rubric changes

I read PR #157, its comments/reviews/review comments, the linked issue #155, maintainer signals, and the existing rubric files. The PR conversation was bot-authored, and the human input in #155 was a concrete product default-value request, not a durable reusable rubric. Existing rubrics already cover the relevant implementation expectations like docs sync, surgical changes, simple implementation, and preview/dry-run safety.

title: Default automation mode and project labels on
